ground.what_this_is: This is the 'Living Instrument' settings panel—a dark, glowing workspace where you configure your AI assistant's brain, voice, and behavior as if tuning a high-tech device rather than filling out forms.
ground.what_you_can_do: Navigate the left rail to switch between Brain, Modes, Voice, Roles, and Composition; adjust the GPU resource meter to see if your settings fit your hardware; and toggle advanced knobs like temperature, token limits, and persona casts.

howto.how_to_change: The surface is Settings.tsx; every write goes through the existing config path (suite.py:set_rhm_config) — it is reuse-only over the real config, not a new store.
howto.what: The consolidated Settings control-room where the system's configuration lives.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the live config of every section at a glance and change what's changeable in one place (the model, persona, presence mode, auto-detect, voice ear/engine/path, role bindings); read-only/unavailable settings are shown honestly, never as working.

howto.how_to_change: Mockup-only; no built code path maps to this specific button yet.
howto.what: The close control on the Living Instrument settings panel.
howto.what_you_can_do: Dismiss the entire configuration workspace and return to the main operational view.

howto.how_to_change: The surface is Settings.tsx; the mode logic is defined in suite.py:rhm_modes.
howto.what: The Modes presence control on the Settings rail.
howto.what_you_can_do: Toggle the current presence mode and see how the system's context resolves based on that selection.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the Settings region (Settings.tsx); changes flow through the existing config path (suite.py:set_rhm_config).
howto.what: The composition control on the settings rail.
howto.what_you_can_do: Open the context-ranking configuration panel to tune the AI's behavior parameters.

howto.how_to_change: The surface is Settings.tsx; the adjustment logic maps to the existing config path (suite.py:set_rhm_config) for reuse-only updates.
howto.what: The resource fit knob on the Living Instrument settings panel.
howto.what_you_can_do: Adjust the GPU resource meter to see if your settings fit your hardware; toggle advanced knobs like temperature, token limits, and persona casts.

howto.how_to_change: The surface is Settings.tsx; the underlying logic is the RHM-config path (suite.py:set_rhm_config) which handles role bindings and composition.
howto.what: The 'conscious role' control within the consolidated Settings control-room.
howto.what_you_can_do: Select or adjust the specific behavioral persona (role) the AI assistant is currently operating under, which dictates its voice, presence mode, and decision-making style.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the Settings.tsx region; changing the available options or their behavior is a code change there, surfaced via the existing config path (suite.py:set_rhm_config).
howto.what: The configuration switch on the Living Instrument settings panel.
howto.what_you_can_do: Toggle between different configuration states or views for the AI assistant's brain, voice, and behavior settings.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the Settings region (Settings.tsx); changing it updates the live config path (suite.py:set_rhm_config) via the existing configuration wire.
howto.what: The output token limit control within the RHM configuration panel.
howto.what_you_can_do: Adjust the maximum number of tokens the system is allowed to generate in a single response.

howto.how_to_change: The surface is Settings.tsx; the value is managed via the existing config path (suite.py:set_rhm_config) under the RHM-config feature.
howto.what: The endpoint configuration field within the Living Instrument settings panel.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the provider base URL for the current endpoint and update it if the setting is changeable.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the Settings.tsx region; changes are applied via the existing config path (suite.py:set_rhm_config).
howto.what: The calm-authority control on the settings panel, representing the default right-hand-man persona.
howto.what_you_can_do: View the system's default right-hand-man configuration and toggle its active state.

howto.how_to_change: This is a mockup-only surface; no built code path exists to change it yet. It lives in the Settings.tsx region but is not yet wired to the runtime.
howto.what: The analytic configuration knob within the Living Instrument settings panel.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the precise, low-affect mode settings for the AI assistant's brain and behavior; these are read-only indicators of the current system state.

howto.how_to_change: The surface is Settings.tsx; changes flow through the existing config path (suite.py:set_rhm_config) for voice parameters.
howto.what: The Voice configuration control in the Settings rail.
howto.what_you_can_do: Open the voice settings to read and adjust the system's auditory engine parameters (the ear, engine, and path) and persona casts.

reasons: refcheck (Layer 1, deterministic floor): feature fabrication: maps_to_feature 'RHM-voice' is not an inventory id nor 'proposed'
accuracy jury (Layer 2, SOFT — variance not error): no quorum that the dossier is grounded in the element (1 of 3 draws agreed grounded). Flagged for operator scrutiny.
